mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls at 158543k in latest report
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the 20-day forward return following positive PAYEMS changes is not significantly greater than the return following negative changes (Welch's t-test p > 0.05), or the Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy dominates equity valuations by raising the risk-free discount rate and dampening credit-sensitive spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 5.33% as of latest reading
failure_condition: In walk-forward testing, triggered instances (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 2-year period, or the mean return in triggered periods is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month deceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to achieve a t-statistic > 2.0 in predicting 60-day forward returns in a 5-year rolling regression, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over triggered periods is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals that supply-side capacity utilization is rolling over, leading to negative operating leverage and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines month-over-month during late-cycle expansions, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in advance retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a direct contraction in nominal consumer expenditure, which reduces near-term revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward instances of month-over-month RSAFS declines fail to produce negative 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of occurrences over any 2-year window, or the mean 5-day return in these instances is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of an inflation peak, a three-month slowdown in consumer price index growth (CPIAUCSL) lifts real household income expectations and reduces the equity risk premium, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, periods with negative 3-month CPIAUCSL changes do not produce positive 60-day forward returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the Rank IC of CPIAUCSL changes falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with contracting industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a monetary-policy-induced growth squeeze that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A regression including the FEDFUNDS×INDPRO interaction term fails to show a negative and significant coefficient (t-stat < -2.0) for 20-day forward returns over any 4-year rolling window, or the interaction-driven portfolio fails to outperform a linear combination of the single factors.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2 percentage point or greater increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month window signals labor market slackening that undermines consumer spending forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances (UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months) produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean forward return during these triggers is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
